question: Which Stellar anchors serve a specific country or currency corridor, what do anchor-to-anchor costs depend on, and when should I build my own anchor instead?

golden.answer:
Use `lumenloop.get_project`, `lumenloop.search_content_semantic`, `lumenloop.search_directory` to retrieve current, source-bounded results for this question. Report the source and as-of date. Treat returned rosters, ranks, counts, and current values as observations, not timeless facts.

Stable guardrails that remain in force: Lays out the build-vs-integrate decision: integrating an existing anchor is usually the lower-risk first launch, while building your own anchor requires licensing/compliance, banking/payout relationships, liquidity/float, and SEP/API implementation.
keyFacts: Retrieves source-bounded live results via lumenloop.get_project, lumenloop.search_content_semantic, lumenloop.search_directory and reports their source and as-of date without freezing a roster, rank, count, or current value.

Lays out the build-vs-integrate decision: integrating an existing anchor is usually the lower-risk first launch, while building your own anchor requires licensing/compliance, banking/payout relationships, liquidity/float, and SEP/API implementation.
avoid: Do NOT name a specific anchor-to-country/currency corridor pairing (e.g. 'anchor X currently serves country Y') as currently live without citing a dated source for that pairing.

Do NOT attribute MoneyGram's 200+-country corporate money-transfer footprint (all-rails boilerplate: 'over 200 countries and territories') to its Stellar USDC off-ramp — MoneyGram's own developer docs put the off-ramp at 174 countries (as of 2026-07; marketing rounds to 170+, older SDF material says 180+). Any figure in the 170-180 band traceable to MoneyGram/SDF is acceptable; 185+ or 200+ for the Stellar cash-out network is not supported by any source.
Do NOT confuse SEP-defined APIs with a guarantee of liquidity, licensing, or coverage.
Do NOT collapse this into a last-mile mobile-money payout how-to; this question owns corridor selection, cost structure, and build-vs-integrate.
Do NOT state a specific current TVL, count, roster, rank, or value as a timeless fact.
golden.notes:
2026-06-29: Differentiated to own corridor SELECTION + anchor-to-anchor cost structure + build-vs-integrate; dropped mobile-money lane (now owned by q-crp-regional-offramp-mobilemoney) and added a must_avoid against collapsing into a last-mile payout how-to. MoneyGram Ramps figures (174 off-ramp countries, USDC SEP-10/24, $5-$2,500 off-ramp) re-verified against developer.moneygram.com. Corridor/provider coverage stays monthly-freshness.
Also good if the answer: Names candidate discovery surfaces such as ecosystem directories, anchor docs, or specific anchor quote/info/interactive endpoints to compare corridors. | Avoids giving universal per-transaction costs without querying dated anchor quote sources.
Golden cites: Dated anchor directory or anchor documentation for any named corridor; SEP docs for API flow claims.
Avoid list re-armed 2026-07-03 (todo 826 residual), deep-verified same day per the golden-truth skill (todo 828): dated-citation guard is answer-visible (and satisfiable — matchPartners returns dated, freshness-stamped corridor matches); the conflation trap is triangulated across MoneyGram dev docs (174, exact), MoneyGram marketing (170+), SDF material (180+), and corporate boilerplate (200+ all-rails). Accept any Stellar-off-ramp figure in the 170-180 band with a MoneyGram/SDF source; punish 185+ and 200+ attributed to the Stellar cash-out network (unsourced anywhere). The conflation framing resists rot better than a bare number: the corporate footprint always exceeds off-ramp coverage. Secondary caution: the ON-ramp is only 30+ countries — don't let candidates conflate on-ramp and off-ramp coverage.

corroboration.claim: MoneyGram's Stellar USDC off-ramp covers 174 countries (developer docs, exact) / 170+ (marketing); the 200+ figure is corporate all-rails footprint
